substances.summary: 卡托普利是心血管相关处方药。本条目用于药代/药效时间进程、监测和相互作用学习索引，不构成医疗建议。
substances.dosageRemark: 临床参考剂量兼容旧剂量模型显示：light/common/strong/heavy 仅表示低参考、常见参考、较高或上限参考层级，不表示娱乐性强度，也不等同个体化处方剂量或确定中毒剂量。毒理风险与适应证、年龄、体重、肝肾功能、合并用药、给药途径和监测结果有关。本资料仅用于学习和资料索引，不构成医疗建议，不用于诊断、处方、自行用药或调整剂量；实际用药必须遵循医生医嘱和当地批准说明书。

drugClass: 血管紧张素转换酶抑制剂
indications: Hypertension
Heart failure contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Can cause hypotension, renal impairment, hyperkalemia, cough, and angioedema.
Fetal toxicity warnings apply to renin-angiotensin system medicines.
majorInteractions: Potassium supplements or potassium-sparing diuretics can increase hyperkalemia risk.
NSAIDs can reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal risk in susceptible patients.

notes: Food can reduce absorption; clinical blood pressure response is not identical to plasma Tmax.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical monitoring is not usually plasma-concentration guided.

drugClass: 血管紧张素转换酶抑制剂
indications: Hypertension
Cardiovascular risk-reduction and heart failure contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Can cause hypotension, renal impairment, hyperkalemia, cough, and angioedema.
Fetal toxicity warnings apply.
majorInteractions: Potassium-increasing drugs can increase hyperkalemia risk.
NSAIDs may reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal risk.

tmax.note: Peak ramipril concentrations are rapid; active ramiprilat peaks later.

peakEffect.note: Approximate peak active ramiprilat concentration window.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Effective half-life for accumulation of ramiprilat after repeated dosing.

notes: Ramipril is a prodrug; active ramiprilat kinetics are more relevant to sustained ACE inhibition.

majorWarnings: Can cause headache, hypotension, syncope, and tolerance with continuous nitrate exposure.

notes: Nitrate tolerance and nitrate-free interval are clinical concepts separate from Tmax.
Extended-release formulations are designed for longer antianginal coverage.

substances.name: Simvastatin
toxicities: Clinically important toxicity depends on indication, dose context, organ function, interacting drugs, and patient-specific risk factors.
substances.summary: 辛伐他汀是心血管相关处方药。本条目用于药代/药效时间进程、监测和相互作用学习索引，不构成医疗建议。
substances.dosageRemark: 临床参考剂量兼容旧剂量模型显示：light/common/strong/heavy 仅表示低参考、常见参考、较高或上限参考层级，不表示娱乐性强度，也不等同个体化处方剂量或确定中毒剂量。毒理风险与适应证、年龄、体重、肝肾功能、合并用药、给药途径和监测结果有关。本资料仅用于学习和资料索引，不构成医疗建议，不用于诊断、处方、自行用药或调整剂量；实际用药必须遵循医生医嘱和当地批准说明书。

indications: Hyperlipidemia and cardiovascular risk-reduction contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Can cause myopathy/rhabdomyolysis and liver enzyme abnormalities; risk increases with interacting drugs and higher dose contexts.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A inhibitors can markedly increase exposure.
Gemfibrozil and other myopathy-risk combinations require caution or avoidance per label.

notes: Simvastatin is a lactone prodrug; lipid response is a longer-term endpoint.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical monitoring is not usually plasma-concentration guided.

substances.name: Ezetimibe
toxicities: Clinically important toxicity depends on indication, dose context, organ function, interacting drugs, and patient-specific risk factors.
substances.summary: 依折麦布是心血管相关处方药。本条目用于药代/药效时间进程、监测和相互作用学习索引，不构成医疗建议。
substances.dosageRemark: 临床参考剂量兼容旧剂量模型显示：light/common/strong/heavy 仅表示低参考、常见参考、较高或上限参考层级，不表示娱乐性强度，也不等同个体化处方剂量或确定中毒剂量。毒理风险与适应证、年龄、体重、肝肾功能、合并用药、给药途径和监测结果有关。本资料仅用于学习和资料索引，不构成医疗建议，不用于诊断、处方、自行用药或调整剂量；实际用药必须遵循医生医嘱和当地批准说明书。

indications: Hyperlipidemia contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Can be associated with liver enzyme abnormalities when combined with statins and rare muscle symptoms.
majorInteractions: Cyclosporine can increase ezetimibe exposure.
Bile acid sequestrants can reduce ezetimibe exposure if timing is not separated.
monitoring: Liver enzymes when combined with statins and clinically indicated

tmax.note: Approximate Tmax for parent ezetimibe; ezetimibe-glucuronide peaks earlier in labeling.

notes: Lipid response is a longer-term endpoint and should not be equated with plasma Tmax.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical monitoring is not usually plasma-concentration guided.

toxicities: Clinically important toxicity depends on indication, dose context, organ function, interacting drugs, and patient-specific risk factors.
substances.summary: 替格瑞洛是心血管相关处方药。本条目用于药代/药效时间进程、监测和相互作用学习索引，不构成医疗建议。
substances.dosageRemark: 临床参考剂量兼容旧剂量模型显示：light/common/strong/heavy 仅表示低参考、常见参考、较高或上限参考层级，不表示娱乐性强度，也不等同个体化处方剂量或确定中毒剂量。毒理风险与适应证、年龄、体重、肝肾功能、合并用药、给药途径和监测结果有关。本资料仅用于学习和资料索引，不构成医疗建议，不用于诊断、处方、自行用药或调整剂量；实际用药必须遵循医生医嘱和当地批准说明书。

indications: Acute coronary syndrome and selected cardiovascular risk contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Bleeding can be serious or fatal; dyspnea and bradyarrhythmia-related events may occur.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A inhibitors can increase ticagrelor exposure.
Strong CYP3A inducers can reduce exposure.
Other antithrombotics or NSAIDs can increase bleeding risk.
monitoring: Heart rate symptoms when clinically indicated

eliminationHalfLife.note: Approximate half-life for ticagrelor and active metabolite.

notes: Platelet inhibition and bleeding risk are pharmacodynamic outcomes distinct from plasma Tmax.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical monitoring is not usually plasma-concentration guided.

indications: Acute coronary syndrome with PCI contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Bleeding can be serious or fatal; risk depends on age, body weight, history of stroke/TIA, procedures, and interacting drugs.
majorInteractions: Other antithrombotics or NSAIDs can increase bleeding risk.
CYP interactions are less central than with some alternatives but clinical context matters.

notes: Platelet inhibition is not the same concept as plasma Tmax.

indications: Stroke/systemic embolism risk reduction and venous thromboembolism contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Bleeding can be serious or fatal. Renal function affects exposure and labeled use.
majorInteractions: P-gp inhibitors can increase exposure in some contexts.
Other anticoagulants, antiplatelets, or NSAIDs can increase bleeding risk.

notes: Routine plasma concentration monitoring is not typical; selected anti-Xa/drug-calibrated testing may be used in specific scenarios.

indications: Venous thromboembolism treatment/prophylaxis and selected ACS contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Bleeding can be serious or fatal. Renal impairment can increase exposure and bleeding risk.
majorInteractions: Other anticoagulants, antiplatelets, or NSAIDs can increase bleeding risk.
Neuraxial anesthesia/spinal puncture can increase spinal hematoma risk.

peakEffect.note: Peak anti-Xa activity after subcutaneous administration.

notes: Anti-Xa activity is an anticoagulant activity measure, not routine plasma concentration monitoring.

indications: Chronic angina contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Can prolong QT interval; renal/hepatic impairment and interacting drugs affect risk.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A inhibitors can increase exposure and may be contraindicated per label.
CYP3A inducers can reduce exposure.
Other QT-prolonging contexts require caution.

notes: Antianginal response and QT effects are pharmacodynamic outcomes distinct from plasma Tmax.
